title: Attribute types should carry an 'Attribute' suffix
messageFormat: Rename attribute type '{0}' so its name ends with 'Attribute'.
summary: A type that derives from System.Attribute is consumed through the attribute usage syntax, where the compiler lets callers drop the trailing Attribute word. Keeping that word in the declared name is what makes the shorthand readable and keeps the declaration consistent with how the framework's own attributes are spelled.
